reactionOptions.text: The state government says murder and conspiracy charges will continue to be vigorously pursued regardless of the POTA commission's finding.

risk.badOutcome: Hindu nationalist organisations criticise the government for not appealing the commission's decision more forcefully.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition and civil-liberties groups say the finding vindicates their long-standing concern that POTA was invoked without meeting its own legal threshold.

risk.badOutcome: The point is disputed by those who argue the underlying case remains serious regardless of which specific law applies.

reactionOptions.text: All parties agree the ordinary criminal trial should proceed expeditiously regardless of the POTA finding.

risk.badOutcome: The ordinary trial's own timeline continues to stretch well beyond initial expectations.

reactionOptions.text: The state Law Department declines to say whether it will formally challenge the commission's finding.

newsItems.title: Assembly Monsoon Session Debates Communal Harmony and Development Balance (2005-06)
newsItems.description: The Assembly's monsoon session sees renewed debate over whether the state's development-focused messaging has been matched by adequate efforts at communal reconciliation in riot-affected neighbourhoods, three years on from the violence. Both sides use the session to stake out positions ahead of continued national scrutiny of the state's record.

reactionOptions.text: The government points to reconstruction of damaged religious structures and continued relief disbursement as evidence of ongoing reconciliation efforts.

risk.badOutcome: Residents in some affected neighbourhoods say meaningful reconciliation efforts, distinct from basic reconstruction, remain limited.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition legislators demand a dedicated communal harmony programme with clear metrics, not just reconstruction spending.

risk.badOutcome: The government says such a programme risks becoming symbolic without a clear implementation mechanism.

reactionOptions.text: An all-party committee agrees to visit riot-affected neighbourhoods together and report back with joint recommendations.

risk.badOutcome: The committee's visit produces a report with recommendations that are only partially acted upon.

reactionOptions.text: The Social Welfare Department declines to give a specific figure for how much has been spent on communal reconciliation efforts distinct from general reconstruction.

newsItems.title: Heavy Monsoon Rains Test Urban Drainage in Ahmedabad and Surat (2005-07)
newsItems.description: Unusually heavy monsoon rainfall causes serious waterlogging in parts of Ahmedabad and Surat, exposing gaps in urban drainage infrastructure even as the state continues to promote its industrial growth narrative to investors. The flooding renews debate over whether infrastructure spending has kept pace with the state's rapid urbanisation.

reactionOptions.text: Municipal authorities deploy emergency pumping equipment and announce a review of drainage capacity in the worst-affected areas.

risk.badOutcome: Waterlogging recurs within days in some of the same low-lying neighbourhoods.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition municipal councillors say rapid industrial growth has outpaced basic civic infrastructure investment in fast-growing cities.

risk.badOutcome: The state points to substantial ongoing drainage investment already underway, disputing the neglect framing.

reactionOptions.text: Municipal and state legislators agree on a joint audit of drainage infrastructure gaps across the state's fastest-growing cities.

risk.badOutcome: The audit's recommendations are costly enough that funding them fully proves difficult.

reactionOptions.text: Municipal authorities decline to give a timeline for completing the drainage capacity review.

newsItems.title: Independence Day Address Emphasises Investment Gains Over Three Years (2005-08)
newsItems.description: Modi's Independence Day address emphasises the cumulative investment secured through two Vibrant Gujarat summits and continuing infrastructure projects, framing the state as a national model for governance-led growth. Opposition parties counter with their own scorecard emphasising unresolved riot cases and continuing questions over encounter killings.

reactionOptions.text: Modi's address highlights cumulative investment, infrastructure and industrial job creation as the state's central achievements over three years.

risk.badOutcome: Independent labour data suggests job creation has lagged behind the investment figures cited.

reactionOptions.text: Congress issues its own scorecard highlighting the Best Bakery case, the Pandya murder and the Ishrat Jahan encounter as the period's unresolved reckonings.

risk.badOutcome: The counter-scorecard struggles for coverage against the more celebratory tone of Independence Day programming.

reactionOptions.text: Both sides agree to a joint fact-check exercise on the job-creation figures cited in the Independence Day address.

risk.badOutcome: The fact-check exercise itself becomes a new point of dispute over methodology.

reactionOptions.text: The Chief Minister's office declines to provide the underlying data behind the job-creation figures cited in the address.

newsItems.title: Legal Scrutiny of Encounter Killings Pattern Continues to Build (2005-09)
newsItems.description: More than a year after the Ishrat Jahan case, legal petitions and rights-group reports continue to accumulate around a broader pattern of police encounter killings attributed to specialised units within the Gujarat police, keeping the issue alive in both the courts and public discourse even as no definitive judicial finding has yet been reached on any individual case.

reactionOptions.text: The Home Department continues to defend individual operations as legitimate counter-terrorism actions taken on credible intelligence.

risk.badOutcome: A serving police officer's internal complaint about specific operations becomes the basis for a fresh legal petition.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition legislators renew calls for an independent, time-bound inquiry covering all disputed encounter cases from the preceding years.

risk.badOutcome: The government again declines to commission a comprehensive inquiry, preferring case-by-case judicial handling.

reactionOptions.text: An all-party legal panel agrees to compile a shared list of disputed cases to be forwarded to the courts for independent review.

risk.badOutcome: Compiling the list proves contentious as parties disagree over which cases genuinely warrant inclusion.

reactionOptions.text: The Home Department declines to say how many active police officers are currently facing legal scrutiny over past operations.

newsItems.title: State Announces Expanded Special Investment Region Policy (2005-10)
newsItems.description: Building on the momentum of successive Vibrant Gujarat summits, the government announces plans for large special investment regions along industrial corridors, aiming to attract capital-intensive projects in petrochemicals and manufacturing. Farmers' groups in some proposed corridor areas raise early concerns about land acquisition terms.

reactionOptions.text: The Industries Department unveils the special investment region policy, projecting substantial new industrial capacity along key corridors.

risk.badOutcome: Farmers in several proposed corridor villages say they were not adequately consulted before the policy's announcement.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition legislators and farmers' groups demand a transparent, negotiated land-acquisition process rather than an administratively imposed one.

risk.badOutcome: The government says the existing acquisition process already includes adequate consultation mechanisms.

reactionOptions.text: An all-party committee agrees to review land-acquisition compensation terms for the proposed investment regions before final approvals.

risk.badOutcome: The review process delays some project timelines, drawing separate industry criticism.

reactionOptions.text: The Industries Department declines to detail specific compensation rates for farmers in the proposed investment corridors.

newsItems.title: Sohrabuddin Sheikh and Wife Kausar Bi Killed in Disputed Police Encounter (2005-11)
newsItems.description: Gujarat and Rajasthan police intercept alleged gangster Sohrabuddin Sheikh and his wife Kausar Bi travelling by bus in late November; days later, Sohrabuddin is killed in a police encounter near Ahmedabad, and Kausar Bi is separately found to have been murdered, her body burned. The case will grow over the following decade into one of the most significant investigations into alleged staged encounters in India, eventually implicating senior police officers and, years later, a serving national political figure who was acquitted after a long trial.

reactionOptions.text: State police say Sohrabuddin was a dangerous armed criminal killed while trying to escape custody, and that his wife's death is being separately investigated.

risk.badOutcome: Sohrabuddin's brother writes to the Chief Justice of India alleging the encounter was staged, prompting the Supreme Court to order the state's own CID to investigate.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition legislators and rights groups demand an independent, non-state investigation into both deaths, citing the unexplained circumstances of Kausar Bi's killing.

risk.badOutcome: The state initially resists an outside probe, saying its own police are capable of investigating the matter.

reactionOptions.text: An all-party call is made for the matter to be placed before the Supreme Court for independent oversight of the investigation.

risk.badOutcome: The investigation, once it begins, takes years to reach any conclusions, keeping the matter unresolved for a long period.

reactionOptions.text: State police decline to explain the circumstances of Kausar Bi's death or confirm who was in custody at the time.

newsItems.title: Supreme Court Orders CID Investigation Into Sohrabuddin Killing (2005-12)
newsItems.description: Acting on the letter from Sohrabuddin Sheikh's brother, the Supreme Court directs Gujarat's Criminal Investigation Department to take over the probe from the police unit originally involved, a first step in what will become a long-running, high-profile investigation stretching across the following decade. The order marks a rare instance of the state's own police being displaced from investigating one of its own operations.

reactionOptions.text: The state government says it welcomes the CID probe and will cooperate fully to establish the facts of the case.

risk.badOutcome: The CID's own findings, once they emerge, point toward the direct involvement of senior serving police officers.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition legislators say the Supreme Court's intervention was necessary given what they call a pattern of the state failing to credibly investigate its own police units.

risk.badOutcome: The government disputes the characterisation of a broader pattern, saying each case should be assessed on its own facts.

reactionOptions.text: All parties agree the CID investigation should be given full independence and adequate resources to complete its work thoroughly.

risk.badOutcome: Questions later arise about whether the CID probe had sufficient independence from the same state police hierarchy.

reactionOptions.text: The Home Department declines to say whether any officers have been reassigned or placed on leave pending the investigation's outcome.
